mnemonics.title: Process States
mnemonics.content: NEW READY RUNNING WAITING TERMINATED - NEVER READY TO WORK ALWAYS? TRY REMEMBER AS: NEW→READY→RUNNING→WAITING→TERMINATED

mnemonics.title: Deadlock Conditions
mnemonics.content: M.H.N.C - MUTUAL EXCLUSION, HOLD AND WAIT, NO PREEMPTION, CIRCULAR WAIT - REMEMBER AS: MY HUNGRY NEIGHBOR CANT EAT

mnemonics.title: CPU Scheduling Criteria
mnemonics.content: MAXIMIZE: CPU UTILIZATION, THROUGHPUT | MINIMIZE: TURNAROUND TIME, WAITING TIME, RESPONSE TIME - REMEMBER AS: FAST RESPONSE (TURNAROUND + WAITING + RESPONSE)

mnemonics.title: SJF Scheduling
mnemonics.content: SHORTEST JOB FIRST - ALWAYS CHOOSES THE PROCESS WITH MINIMUM BURST TIME - THINK: SERVE THE QUICKEST CUSTOMER FIRST FOR LESS WAITING IN QUEUE

mnemonics.title: Process vs Thread
mnemonics.content: PROCESS = INDEPENDENT (OWN MEMORY) | THREAD = SHARES WITH SIBLINGS (LIGHTWEIGHT) - REMEMBER: THREADS ARE FAMILY MEMBERS SHARING HOUSE, PROCESSES ARE STRANGERS LIVING SEPARATE

acronyms.usage: Data structure storing all process information like state, PC, registers, memory info
The main processor that executes process instructions
Non-preemptive scheduling based on arrival time
Scheduling based on burst time (shortest first)
Preemptive version of SJF, also called SRTF
Preemptive scheduling with fixed time quantum
Time from process arrival to completion
Time process spends in ready queue for CPU
Mechanism for processes to communicate and synchronize

visualTips.visualization: DRAW A CIRCLE WITH 5 STATES (NEW→READY→RUNNING→WAITING→TERMINATED) AND ARROWS SHOWING TRANSITIONS LIKE NEW→READY (admitted), READY→RUNNING (scheduler dispatch), RUNNING→READY (interrupt), RUNNING→WAITING (I/O request), WAITING→READY (I/O complete), RUNNING→TERMINATED (exit)
THINK OF TWO BOOKS (PROCESSES) AND YOU MARK YOUR PAGE (SAVE STATE) IN ONE BEFORE OPENING THE OTHER - THE BOOKMARK REPRESENTS PCB SAVING
IMAGINE A LINE OF PEOPLE (PROCESSES) WAITING FOR A TAXI (CPU). FIRST IN LINE GETS THE TAXI FIRST (FCFS). OR THEY ARE ARRANGED BY TICKET NUMBER (PRIORITY).
THINK OF A FERRIS WHEEL (CPU) WITH SEATS (TIME SLICES). EACH PERSON (PROCESS) GETS ONE RIDE THEN MOVES TO BACK OF LINE.
TWO PEOPLE HOLDING ONE FORK EACH AND WAITING FOR THE OTHER FORK TO EAT - NEITHER CAN PROCEED (CIRCULAR WAIT + HOLD AND WAIT)
DRAW A HOUSE (PROCESS) WITH MULTIPLE ROOMS (THREADS). ALL ROOMS SHARE THE KITCHEN AND LIVING ROOM (CODE/DATA) BUT HAVE THEIR OWN BEDROOMS (REGISTERS/STACK).

analogies.analogy: A PROCESS IS LIKE A RECIPE BEING COOKED - THE RECIPE BOOK IS THE PROGRAM, AND THE COOKING ACTIVITY IS THE PROCESS IN EXECUTION.
PCB IS LIKE A STUDENT'S REPORT CARD THAT KEEPS TRACK OF THEIR CURRENT STATUS, SCORES, ATTENDANCE, AND PERSONAL INFO.
CPU SCHEDULER IS LIKE A TEACHER DECIDING WHICH STUDENT (FROM THE READY QUEUE) GETS TO SPEAK (USE CPU) NEXT.
CONTEXT SWITCH IS LIKE WHEN YOU SAVE YOUR VIDEO GAME PROGRESS AND LOAD ANOTHER GAME - YOUR STATE IS SAVED AND OTHER STATE IS LOADED.
CRITICAL SECTION IS LIKE A SINGLE TOILET IN AN OFFICE - ONLY ONE PERSON CAN USE IT AT A TIME TO AVOID EMBARRASSMENT.
THREAD POOL IS LIKE A TEAM OF WAITERS IN A RESTAURANT - TASKS (CUSTOMER ORDERS) ARE ASSIGNED TO AVAILABLE WAITERS (THREADS) RATHER THAN HIRING NEW ONES EACH TIME.
DEADLOCK IS LIKE TWO PEOPLE TRYING TO PASS EACH OTHER IN A NARROW HALLWAY - EACH STEPS ASIDE IN THE SAME DIRECTION AND THEY BLOCK EACH OTHER INDEFINITELY.
TIME QUANTUM IN ROUND ROBIN IS LIKE A TIMER IN A SPEECH CONTEST - EACH SPEAKER (PROCESS) GETS EQUAL TIME BEFORE BEING ASKED TO STOP.
